summary: Soundboard recording of good quality captures a mixed‑bag show – strong moments like Dark Star, Eternity and The Corrina contrast with uneven jams and a weary Jerry.
review: The SBD source is generally crisp, fat and lush, though a few listeners note distant snare miking and occasional mix quirks. Musically the concert swings between highlights (a soaring Dark Star, an intense Eternity, bouncy The Corrina and a solid Terrapin Station) and weaker spots (disappointing post‑Terrapin jam, a lackluster FOTM solo and signs of Jerry’s fatigue). Overall fans are divided, with many praising Branford Marsalis’ contributions while others feel the band never quite clicked.

ai_rating.rationale: 11 reviews provide a clear picture: recording quality is consistently praised (good‑to‑excellent), but performance ratings range from 1 to 5 stars, yielding an average around 3.5–3.8 after weighting show quality more heavily.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp and lush SBD sound praised by several reviewers; a few note distant snare mic placement and minor mix imbalances but no major drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Setlist is uneven – the first set and early second‑set attempts feel tentative, while the Dark Star/ETERNITY peak gives the concert its high points. Some listeners felt the show never fully gelled, others called it a true gem.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Classic tone and vocal inflection praised by several fans; however, other reviewers note he sounded off, weary and occasionally out of sync with Branford.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s solo in FOTM described as weak; otherwise keyboards receive little specific commentary.
band_member_comments.Drums: One reviewer mentions the snare sounding distant (possible mic issue); no major drumming complaints beyond tempo feel.
